data.narrative:
Zap Energy is a Series D nuclear energy company founded by Benj Conway, Brian A. Nelson, and Uri Shumlak, currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $330M+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 11 peer companies across the nuclear energy sector—3 public and 8 private—including TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, Nano Nuclear Energy, and Type One Energy, with peer valuations ranging from $200M to $4.0B.

Two methodologies inform this analysis. Stage-progression modeling applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $1.5B. Peer-median benchmarking across 6 same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers produces a median of $1.6B, anchored by Kairos Power at $1.6B and Nano Nuclear Energy at $1.5B. The peer set exhibits substantial dispersion, reflecting varying technology maturity and capital structures within advanced nuclear.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.8B, with a median of $1.5B. This implies modest upside from the current $1.0B valuation. Key uncertainties include capital intensity and regulatory pathway clarity, both material drivers of stage-progression multiples in this sector, and the marked valuation spread within the peer set, which suggests heterogeneous investor expectations around commercialization timelines and technology viability.
